note: APPROVED DESIGN DIRECTION (session 2026-07-02): the stage/spotlight interaction, prototyped and approved by the human at prototypes/unfold-v3-stage.html. That file is the design contract — open it in a browser before building anything. Core thesis: canvas coordinates remain the single spatial truth, but stage mode is a second PROJECTION of the same graph — focused group renders center-stage at readable size; external connections compress into directional proxy pills positioned by TRUE direction (from group centroids in ctx.state), abolishing travel cost while preserving spatial meaning. DO NOT REGRESS TO: whole-canvas-always-visible, instant DOM-swap reveals, wires as an off-by-default toggle, forced pixel travel to distant nodes. Motion is part of the contract: staggered fade-up entrances (~55ms stagger, ~650ms expo ease), wires draw themselves in after cards land, stage entrance slides from travel direction, background world blurs/fades when staged. Builds on top of unfold-integration.plan.json (that plan's changes remain valid). Resume: npm run flowmap:status -- --plan docs/flowmap/plans/unfold-v3-stage.plan.json

phases.title: motion + focus land in reading mode
phases.subtitle: staggered reveal, focus illumination, reframe-to-fit

phases.title: illumination completes
phases.subtitle: type-level focus across the whole surface

intent.problem: Unfold reveals are instant DOM swaps — jumpy, the opposite of the approved premium feel.
intent.approach: enterStagger(): newly-revealed cards enter with staggered fade-up (born → in class pattern, ~55ms stagger, ~650ms cubic-bezier(.16,1,.3,1)); wires draw in via stroke-dash animation only after their endpoint cards have landed. Reference implementation in prototypes/unfold-v3-stage.html (renderStructure/born/wireEnterAt).

intent.problem: Selecting a node highlights it but everything else stays at full strength — no illumination, connections do not visibly light up, the wiring story is lost in noise.
intent.approach: focusDim(): on select, selected card glows (accent ring + soft shadow), 1-hop neighbours stay lit, its wires go accent with animated dash-flow (visible plumbing flow), everything else dims to ~15-25% opacity. Esc clears. CSS-class-driven, no rebuild.

intent.problem: As the reader unfolds, content outgrows the viewport; legacy answer was manual pan/zoom travel.
intent.approach: reframeToFit(): after every structural change, the world container transform-scales/translates so all visible content fits, animated ~900ms expo ease (the canvas breathes). Reuses camera zoomToFit semantics against the unfold surface.

intent.problem: With many groups unfolded the surface reverts to the legacy whole-canvas problem: too much on screen, focus diluted, distant connections force travel.
intent.approach: stageMode(): clicking a module projects its GROUP to center stage at full readable size; the explore surface stays behind, blurred (~5px) and faded (~16% opacity). Same graph, second projection — no new model state beyond spec.stage. Exit via Esc / explore button restores prior explore state exactly.

intent.problem: Staging a group must not amputate its external wiring — the reader needs to see WHERE connections go without traveling to them.
intent.approach: stageProxies(): the selection's external edges aggregate per target group into pill proxies on a ring around the stage; each pill's position derives from the TRUE angle between group centroids in ctx.state positions (the human's manual layout becomes the source of proxy directions). Curved wires from selected card to pills; selected wires carry the flow animation; advisory edges marked.

intent.problem: Seeing a distant connection should offer more detail and optional relocation — never a 1000px pan.
intent.approach: stageTravel(): click a proxy → peek (pill expands in place to mini-card: module names + desc); explicit travel action → target group slides onto stage FROM its direction, origin group becomes a proxy in the reciprocal direction (reciprocity is automatic since both derive from the same centroids).

intent.problem: Frontmatter types (accepts/returns/state) are dead text; the approved magic moment is clicking a type and watching every carrier illuminate across the surface.
intent.approach: typeFocus(): types in frontmatter cards are clickable; on click, every module whose frontmatter carries that type lights (accent ring), all else dims, inspector lists carriers. Derives from the real frontmatter data (ctx.state), not a parallel index.
